author: romelium
description: This simplifies creating and visualizing timelines from text using dates and metadata.

manifest.author: Romelium
manifest.description: Create beautiful visual timelines from your notes using natural language dates (e.g., 'yesterday', 'next week'). Customize events with inline metadata, icons, and statuses. Supports relative dates via frontmatter properties and timezones.
manifest.name: Easy Timeline

name: Easy Timeline

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Easy Timeline 2.2.1 Release Notes

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* **Reading View & Embeds:** Fixed an issue where settings placed directly on the code block language line (e.g., ```` ```timeline sort:desc ````) were ignored when viewing the timeline in Reading View or within an embedded note.
* **Indented Code Blocks:** Fixed an issue where timeline blocks failed to render or parse correctly if they were indented (e.g., placed inside lists, blockquotes, or callouts).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Romelium/obsidian-easy-timeline/compare/2.2.0...2.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Easy Timeline 2.2.0 Release Notes

### ✨ Features
* **Preserve Timezones:** Introduced a setting for showing the date and time exactly as they were written, including the corresponding timezones such as `(EST)` or `(JST)`.
* **Sort Relative to Timezone:** Introduced a new sorting method where events are sorted based on their local time rather than their global time, taking into account timezone differences.
* **Extensive Timezone Mapping:** Introduced a large dataset of global timezone abbreviations (including South American, African, Asian, and other Pacific areas) to increase the accuracy of date parsing.
* **Automatic Re-rendering of Settings:** Timeline blocks will now automatically re-render when you make changes to your settings. The new settings will be applied instantly to your notes without having to manually refresh them.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Easy Timeline 2.1.0 Release Notes

### ✨ Time Management and UI Enhancements

In this release, we are working to ensure that our timeline cards are more intelligent and cleaner when displaying dates without time information.

* **No Default Time for Vague Dates:** When you specify vague dates like `"Tomorrow"` or `"Dec 31"`, no default time value will be shown in the corner of the timeline card. However, if you do mention a time (such as `"Today at 2:30 PM"`), it will be shown.
* **Clean Date Format:** In case there is a time in the timeline, it will be neatly formatted instead of the old, cluttered format (`2:30 PM`/`14:30`). We no longer see `14:30:00` in the timeline card.
* **Fallback Title from Date Text:** If the timeline does not explicitly have a title or any markdown heading, the timeline title bar will take the capitalized version of date text (for example, **"Tomorrow"**).
* **Fixing Collapsing Title Bar:** If the grey title bar had nothing but empty space, it would collapse in a strange way. This is now fixed, and all timeline cards look neat and consistent.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Romelium/obsidian-easy-timeline/compare/2.0.0...2.1.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Easy Timeline 2.0.0 Release Notes

## 🎉 Main Features
* **Markdown Support**: Details for Timeline events have full support for native Obsidian Markdown formatting capabilities. Rich text formatting, hyperlinks, images, videos and other Markdown entities may now be used in Timeline boxes.
* **Live Updates**: Timelines now update live as you edit metadata or the document content without having to switch between views or refresh the note page.
* **Theme Compatibility**: The entire plugin has been redesigned using native Obsidian CSS variables (replacing Bootstrap). This means that Timelines will perfectly adjust to the current selected theme, whether it is Light or Dark.
* **Embedding Support**: Embedding a Timeline into another note now works without any glitches. Metadata and data retrieval from source files will now work regardless of the embedding note.

## ✨ Settings & Configuration
* **Block & Inline Metadata Settings**: You may add the `sort` and `reference` metadata settings either directly in the language line of the code block (e.g., ` ```timeline sort:desc reference:"2024-01-01" `) or in Dataview-like form inside or outside the block.
* **Date Reference Point**: Introduced a feature that lets you decide whether your reference date point should be based on the file's **Creation Date** or **Last Modified Date**, in the absence of any metadata.

## 🎨 Style Tweaks
* **Tight Spacing**: Tightened the spacing between timeline months, sections, and their corresponding content for a sleeker design.
* **End Marker**: Added an end-point circular marker at the bottom of the timeline.
* **Unique Styles**: Changed generic container CSS class names to isolate styles for timeline elements from potential clashes with other plugins.

## 🔧 Bugs and Under-the-Hood Fixes
* **Event Count Fix**: Corrected the month header display to show event count rather than number of distinct days in a month.
* **Parsing Enhancements**:
  * Enhanced regex for extracting keys and values from metadata.
  * Fixed bugs that could confuse the plugin into considering code blocks without metadata.
  * Supported tilde (`~~~`) delimiter style code blocks and accounted for stale section data due to edits above the block.
* **Recursive Handling**: Prevented multiple timeline blocks within one file from causing recursion or infinite loops.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Romelium/obsidian-easy-timeline/compare/1.1.0...2.0.0
